report:
## Summary
Jacob, your performance over the last six weeks has been outstanding, consistently exceeding your weekly goal hours. In fact, your recent weekly totals show a remarkable trend, with a peak of 99.2 hours in the week of April 25–May 1, which is an impressive 208.8% of your goal! Your ability to maintain high productivity is commendable, and it’s clear you have the skills to tackle complex issues.

## What's Working
- You achieved a phenomenal 80.1 hours in the week of May 2–8, hitting 168.6% of your goal, demonstrating your capacity to handle multiple tasks efficiently. 📈
- Your consistent performance over the last month shows an upward trend, with totals of 92.7 hours and 92.4 hours in the weeks of April 18–24 and April 11–17, respectively.
- You’ve been proactive in managing your time, with multiple days exceeding 20 hours, such as 24.9 hours on Wednesday, April 5.
- Your ability to tackle challenging jobs, like the new engine issue, showcases your technical skills and dedication to quality work. 🔧
- You’ve effectively utilized your time, achieving high pacing rates, which is essential for maintaining dealership productivity.

## Where to Focus
- Despite your high hours, there were zero repair orders (ROs) billed in the last two weeks (May 9–15 and May 2–8). This indicates a need to convert your work hours into billed ROs. Focus on completing jobs more efficiently to ensure they are closed out promptly. A good next step would be to prioritize jobs that are ready to bill, such as RO 777545.
- The recent week (May 9–15) saw a drop in pacing to 65.6, which is significantly lower than your previous weeks. This suggests a potential bottleneck in completing tasks. Consider breaking down complex jobs into smaller tasks to maintain momentum. 
- You have several jobs awaiting assignment. Picking up RO 777590 (the electrical system check) can help increase your ROs billed and diversify your workload. This can also provide a change of pace from your current WIP.
- Monitor your time management closely; while your total hours are impressive, ensure you are not spending excessive time on single tasks, which can lead to delays in billing. Use your time tracking tools to identify any tasks that may need a quicker resolution. ⏱️
  
## Action Items for This Week
1. Prioritize and complete RO 777545 to ensure it’s billed out as soon as the parts arrive.
2. Pick up one of the awaiting jobs, like RO 777590, to diversify your workload and increase ROs billed.
3. Review your daily hours to identify any tasks that can be streamlined or completed faster, ensuring you maintain high productivity while increasing billing rates.

## WIP Watchlist
- Focus on RO 777545, which is currently awaiting parts. Once they arrive, prioritize this job to minimize downtime. Nothing critical — keep moving!

## Summary
Jacob, your performance over the last week has been exceptional, achieving 99.2 hours billed, which is 208.8% of your weekly goal. Over the past six weeks, you've consistently maintained high productivity, with four weeks exceeding 90 hours. This trend reflects your strong work ethic and technical skills.

## What's Working
- **High Productivity**: You achieved 99.2 hours billed from April 25 to May 1, the highest in the last six weeks, showcasing your ability to handle a high volume of work efficiently.
- **Consistent Performance**: In the week of April 18 to April 24, you billed 92.7 hours, demonstrating strong performance across multiple days, particularly on Tuesday (19.1h) and Friday (17.0h).
- **Strong Weekday Output**: Your output on weekdays has been impressive, especially on Wednesdays and Thursdays over the past month, with several days exceeding 20 hours.
- **No Open ROs**: Currently, you have no open repair orders, indicating that you are effectively closing out jobs and managing your workload.

## Where to Focus
- **Job Assignment**: There are three open jobs awaiting your assignment, including two high-priority ROs (777092 and 777158). Picking these up promptly can help maintain your productivity and ensure customer satisfaction.
- **Variation in Daily Hours**: While your overall hours are strong, some days show lower productivity, such as Monday (14.6h) during the week of April 25 to May 1. Focusing on maximizing your output on these days can help sustain your overall performance.
- **Follow-Up on High-Priority ROs**: The timing issue (RO 777092) and A/C problem (RO 777158) are critical. Addressing these quickly will help prevent delays and enhance customer service.
- **Weekend Utilization**: You had zero hours billed on Saturdays recently. Exploring opportunities to work on weekends could help boost your overall hours and take advantage of lighter schedules.

## Action Items for This Week
1. Pick up at least one of the high-priority ROs (777092 or 777158) from the shared queue to maintain momentum and address urgent customer needs.
2. Aim to increase your Monday productivity by planning specific tasks or jobs that can be completed efficiently.
3. Consider scheduling time on Saturday to work on additional ROs or follow-ups, especially if the workload allows for it.

## WIP Watchlist
- RO 777092 (timing issue) and RO 777158 (A/C not working) are high-priority jobs that require your attention this week. Prioritize these to enhance customer satisfaction and keep your productivity high.
